title: Discovery log initialized
summary: Initial discovery_log.json created. Real discoveries, validated hypotheses, rejected hypotheses, and verification events will be appended automatically by the Autonomous Research Agent.

fields_help.title: Short human readable label for the discovery or event
fields_help.summary: Two to four sentence description of what was found or decided
fields_help.run_id: Logical run or experiment id, for example run_001 or longevity_scout_01
fields_help.domain: Research domain tag, for example longevity, math, biology, ai_safety, general
fields_help.rye_gain: Change in RYE attributed to this discovery or validation (float). Positive means better repair efficiency.
fields_help.delta_rye: Optional alias for rye_gain, also a float.
fields_help.delta_R: Change in repair score or stability metric associated with this event.
fields_help.energy: Approximate energy or effort cost for this discovery in the TGRM loop.
fields_help.agent_role: Role of the agent that produced the discovery, for example researcher, critic, theorist, explorer, integrator, swarm_3.
fields_help.cycle_index: Index of the cycle in the global history where this discovery was logged.
fields_help.tags: List of string tags, for example ["candidate", "mechanism", "longevity", "high_confidence"].
fields_help.sources: Optional list of key sources or citations, each item like {"title": "...", "url": "...", "type": "pubmed|web|pdf"}.
fields_help.notes: Free form extra notes for humans or downstream tools.
fields_help.verification_score: If the item came from the verification engine this is its final score between 0 and 1.
fields_help.hypothesis_id: Link back to the originating hypothesis record when applicable.

title: Example discovery placeholder
summary: This is a placeholder discovery used only to show the JSON shape. Real discoveries from the swarm will be appended below this entry during autonomous runs.
notes: You can delete this example entry later. It simply proves that the UI and loader can handle a full discovery object.

title: Example validated hypothesis template
summary: Template entry showing how a validated hypothesis will be recorded. Real validated items are written by the verification engine.
notes: The verification engine will populate verification_score and hypothesis_id with real values.

title: Example inconclusive verification template
summary: Shows how an inconclusive verification attempt is logged. The hypothesis remains pending and can be revisited by later runs.
notes: Real entries of this type help you see which ideas need more data or better modeling.

title: Example rejected hypothesis template
summary: Template entry for a rejected hypothesis. In real runs this signals that an idea failed literature checks, data checks, or swarm critique.
notes: Rejected ideas are still valuable for showing where RYE dropped and why the system corrected course.
